credentials_over_cleartext.description: WSTG-ATHN-01 -- login form submits credentials over http:// instead of https:// (form action attribute, mixed content, http-only auth host)

probes.description: Inspect login response for form action -- if action attribute begins with http:// or omits scheme on a non-HTTPS page, credentials transit cleartext
Login endpoint reachable on http:// -- TLS not enforced for credential POST

auth_response_browser_cacheable.description: WSTG-ATHN-06 -- authentication-protected response lacks Cache-Control: no-store, allowing browser/proxy to cache PII or session state

probes.description: Authenticated response cacheable -- missing Cache-Control: no-store on a response that contains user data leaks via shared-cache / browser-back

session_cookie_missing_secure_flag.description: WSTG-SESS-02 -- session cookie set without Secure / HttpOnly / SameSite. Detect on the cookie-issuing response (login, refresh, session-create)

probes.description: Session cookie issued without Secure flag -- transits over http:// if site downgrade is possible
Session cookie issued without HttpOnly -- accessible to document.cookie / XSS exfil
Session cookie issued without SameSite (or SameSite=None without Secure) -- CSRF + cross-site request leakage

logout_does_not_invalidate_session.description: WSTG-SESS-06 -- after POST /logout, the old session cookie / bearer token still authenticates requests. Stateless JWT without server-side revocation is the classic case.

probes.description: Capture session token, POST /logout, replay protected endpoint with same token -- 200 = no server-side revocation. Run with run_flow: login -> action -> logout -> replay-action.

concurrent_sessions_not_invalidated.description: WSTG-SESS-11 -- re-login from a new device does not invalidate prior session tokens (common in stateless JWT, OAuth refresh-token, mobile-app cookie jars)

probes.description: Capture session A, login again -> session B, replay protected endpoint with session A -- 200 = old session still alive. Useful chain anchor for stolen-token persistence.

session_token_in_url.description: WSTG-SESS-04 -- session ID / auth token transmitted in URL (query string, path segment) -- logged in proxy/server access logs, leaks via Referer, browser history

probes.description: Authenticated request carries token in query/path -- if endpoint accepts and processes the request, token is logged in every upstream
